short_description_of_the_course.label: Short Description of the Course
short_description_of_the_course.value: In this course, students will learn the process for how to design, build, test, deploy, maintain, and monitor scalable and robust data products using the Data Product Life Cycle (DPLC). Students will gain hands-on experience working with datasets and use cases, collaborating in teams, and applying agile methodologies to deliver data products that meet the needs of real world stakeholders. The course will cover the entire DPLC process, including experimentation and productization, with a focus on reliability, fault tolerance, scalability, deployment, and meeting regulatory requirements. The course will prepare students for careers in data & digital technology, equipping them with the knowledge and skills required to work in cross-functional teams and navigate complex regulatory requirements.

requirements_of_entry.label: Requirements of Entry
value: Algorithmic Foundations 2 (or equivalent) is recommended but not required

co_requisites.label: Co-requisites
co_requisites.value: Machine Learning H (or equivalent) is recommended but not required

timetable_and_length_and_frequency_of_te.label: Timetable and length and frequency of teaching sessions
timetable_and_length_and_frequency_of_te.value: 1 hour lecture per week and 2 hours of practical labs per week

course_aims.label: Course Aims
course_aims.value: To ensure the students learn about building large scale collaborative data products in an enterprise environment. The course aims to focus on machine learning and large language model development at scale.

intended_learning_outcomes_of_course.label: Intended Learning Outcomes of Course
intended_learning_outcomes_of_course.value:
By the end of this course students will be able to:

Articulate the phases, workflow and key outputs of the data product life-cycle
Identify when and how to experiment to prove or disprove a hypothesis
Explain how to productionise a successful hypothesis as part of a team across the enterprise
Identify, describe and perform the key roles in the Data Product Life-cycle / Data Product Engineering
Justify data product design choices aligned with business and regulatory requirements

description_of_summative_assessment.label: Description of Summative Assessment
description_of_summative_assessment.value:
10% Weekly in class quiz to cover knowledge.
50% Repeated single slide presentation on different aspects of the product journey based on weekly topics.  *** Each person delivers one slide to be assessed on (developed slide collaboratively) *** 
Development of a hypothesis driven experiment
Presentation of the rationale for the exploring the hypothesis of business benefits (quality improvement, risk reduction, cost savings/avoidance, increased revenue/margin, customer satisfaction)
Link to importance of non-functional criteria
Assess both vision/roadmap and execution towards this in implementation and link to non-functional considerations (fault tolerance, data privacy, security…)
40% On the product itself.  
Assume a dashboard to be developed  
Power BI/Tableaux/Cloud deployment

formative_assessment_feedback.label: Formative Assessment & Feedback
formative_assessment_feedback.value: Continuous feedback from real world stakeholders

additional_relevant_information.label: Additional Relevant Information
additional_relevant_information.value: This course is to be developed within the scope of a Royal Academy of Engineering funded Visiting Professorship (Andrew McDonald 2023-2026).
